content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
October 2, 1994

Jeanne Goodin, Director
Eagle Forum Long Beach Chapter
54th Assembly District
Central Committee
Post Office Box 15942
Long Beach, CA 90815-0942

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-290

Dear Ms. Goodin:

You have asked for confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

May the Eagle Forum Long Beach Chapter (EFLBC), a
nonpartisan, nonprofit organization, co-host a fundraising event
with the 54th Assembly District Central Committee (54ADCC) which
is a committee as defined in the Act?

CONCLUSION

From the information you have provided EFLBC will not become
a committee as defined in the Act and will not incur filing
obligations pursuant to its co-hosting a fundraising event with an
entity that is a committee. However, if EFLBC engages in other
activities which would make it a committee, it will incur filing
obligations.

FACTS

EFLBC is co-hosting a joint fundraising event with the
54ADCC, which is a Republican central committee. It is my
understanding that each organization is sending invitations to its
own members, friends and supporters. Money received from members
will be deposited by each organization into its own separate

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Goodin, Jeanne
File No. A-94-290
Page 2


account. Expenses will be apportioned to each organization and
each will directly pay for its own expenses.

     You indicated in a telephone conversation that any money
raised from this event will be used for the general overhead
expenses of BFLBC and will not be used to make contributions or
expenditures to support or oppose any candidates or committees.
You have also indicated that BFLBC attempts to create greater
awareness of and involvement in various broad social, economic and
political issues without supporting or opposing any specific
candidates. You also indicated that BFLBC does indiate its
support or opposition of ballot measures but does not  make
expenditures or contributions supporting or opposing ballot
measures.

                              ANALYSIS

     There is nothing in the Act which would prevent BFLBC from
participation in a joint fundraising activity with an organization
which qualifies as a committee as defined in the Act. In
addition, this activity in itself will not make BFLBC a committee.

     Section 83013 defines "committee" as:

          ... any person or combination or persons who directly or
          indirectly does any of the following:

               (a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand
          dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year.

               (b) Makes independent expenditures totaling one
          thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year; or

               (c) Makes contributions totaling ten thousand
          dollars ($10,000) or more in a calendar year to or at
          the behest of candidates or committees.

     Thus, an organization only becomes a "committee" if it
receives or makes "contributions," or makes "independent
expenditures." Section 82015 and Regulation 18215 define
"contributions" as:


          ...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a
          loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make
          a payment except to the extent that full and adequate
          consideration is received unless it is clear from the
          surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
          political purposes.

                                    Section 82015.

          (a)...a payment is made for political purposes if it is:

               (1) For the purpose of influencing or
          attempting to influence the action of the voters

Goodin, Jeanne
File No. A-94-290
Page 3


for or against the nomination or election of a
candidate or candidates, or the qualification or
passage of any measure; or

(2) Received by or made at the behest of:

(A) A candidate...;
(B) A controlled committee;
(C) An official committee of a political
party...; or
(D) An organization formed or.existing
primarily for political purposes...

Regulation 18215.

Money raised to support the types of activities in which
EFLBC currently engages are not contributions under the Act and,
therefore, EFLBC will not incur any campaign disclosure
obligations in connection with the joint fundraiser.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5562.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
